channel.title: +MT on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +MT on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Meteoric impacts
item.description:
Source: tw

Was looking at records of meteoritic impacts in India. Wonder how many of the falls have been preserved.

  1. 1800 CE shortly after the English conquest of the tyrant of Mysuru a fall was reported at shrIrangapattana which is was said to be “the size of an elephant”: Exaggeration ?

  2. Dec 19th 1798 CE the vArANasi fall – a loud explosion was heard; several falling bodies were heard crashing to the ground. Another report of objects falling from the sky was reported 14 km from vArANasi. Next day several fragments of a meteorite were recovered. Any survive?

item.title: Reintroducing animals
item.description:
Source: TW

It is good to see the cheetah make its comeback in the desh. Hope that holds. I also suspect that reintroducing the even more remotely extinct (by human hands) ostrich might be a good addition to the ecosystem, especially in Guj and Raj. The Javan rhinoceros, in principle, would be a good addition to the vA~nga country, but it is in too precarious a situation to even think of reintroduction. There is a perverse part of me that wonders about bringing back the hippopotamus. But I guess nobody would think of that, given how much they reengineer riverine ecosystems and the density of human populations on riverbanks. I wonder if they have been reintroduced to Ambani’s forest though.
